examples: Front-drive cars work better in the snow and slush.
In the city the clean white snow had turned to grey slush.
The slush is compacted by an unknown mechanism, and solid, bubble-free ice is formed from water high in soluble organic substances.
Huge blocks of ice calved off and collapsed into tidal waves of icy slush.
Driving conditions were difficult, although much of the snow in urban areas turned to slush.
